summary: NO-SHIP: the 8b2f521 flip record is internally consistent, ancestral, and test-frozen, and R-1/R-2 were handled as stated. However, the mandatory verification document still certifies the old GREEN SHA, and R-3’s single-consumer exception remains recorded as pending outside the Decision Log.

findings.title: Missing verification: post-R-1 GREEN SHA
findings.body: The lock now pins green.sha to 8b2f521 and a matching script-owned record exists, but verification.md still declares b2b3dc0 as GREEN and attributes the PASS evidence to that pre-R-1 tree. Consequently, the mandatory verification artifact does not demonstrate the final locked state after the comment change, despite the raw record being available.
findings.recommendation: Refresh verification.md from bugfix-verify-green-cd6f1c11e15f9b2aa7e50193225d7a23e7a1ca17.json: identify 8b2f521 as GREEN and include the locked regression and characterization command outputs. Do not hand-edit the verify-record.

findings.title: R-3 exception is not recorded as human-approved
findings.body: The R-3 row contains the conductor’s justification, but the following text still labels human approval as pending. Moreover, this release table appears before the actual `## Review Decision Log` section at line 135. Thus the committed branch does not yet contain the human-approved Decision-Log exception required for a shared seam with only one adapter, even though this prompt now supplies that approval.
findings.recommendation: Record the R-3 acceptance under the actual Review Decision Log, remove the pending-confirmation wording, and reference F-5 as the approved gated-refactor follow-up.

next_steps: Hold the merge and correct both committed evidence records.
Because this is Round 2, explicitly triage these residual findings; landing remains blocked unless they are fixed, waived, or a manual Round 3 is approved.
No test or repro rerun is needed solely for these documentation corrections; preserve the existing machine-owned records unless a lock command, harness, or GREEN tree changes.
